result.conflict_reason: The DOC detector found a strong and coherent Introduction section snippet describing the research problem, context, and objectives, while the PDF detector did not find the Introduction section likely due to extraction or layout issues.
result.reason: The DOC snippet clearly outlines the research context and objectives related to the RSG-GAS reactor and its safety analysis report revision, indicating the presence of an Introduction section. The PDF snippet contains only metadata and header information without the Introduction content, suggesting a failure in PDF text extraction or layout parsing.
result.evidence_summary: DOC snippet provides detailed introduction content about the reactor, regulatory context, and study objectives. PDF snippet lacks Introduction content and only shows article metadata and header.
result.evidence: DOC snippet provides detailed introduction content about the reactor, regulatory context, and study objectives. PDF snippet lacks Introduction content and only shows article metadata and header.

verifier_input.doc_snippet: Currently, the Multipurpose Reactor (RSG-GAS), the National Research and Innovation Agency (BRIN), as the Nuclear Installation Operator (NIO) operating the RSG-GAS reactor, will submit a revised Safety Analysis Report (SAR) document to the Nuclear Energy Regulatory Agency (BAPETEN) regarding the reactor's operating permit. In line with the revision process, BAPETEN, as a technical support unit, is conducting an independent assessment of the RSG-GAS reactor's safety related to its supervisory duties. The results of this study are used to provide technical support to the Licensing Directorate in the SAR evaluation process. This study presents the calculation of the RSG-GAS equilibrium core excess reactivity at the Beginning of Cycle (BOC) condition. This is important to do because the BOC eq
